name: Industrial IoT Gateway Compromise
description: Compromise Industrial IoT (IIoT) gateways that bridge OT protocols (Modbus, OPC-UA, BACnet) to cloud platforms via MQTT/AMQP, gaining bidirectional access between IT and OT networks.
root_objective: Compromise IIoT gateways to pivot between IT/OT networks and manipulate industrial data

nodes.title: Compromise IIoT Gateways for IT/OT Pivot and Data Manipulation
nodes.description: Take control of Industrial IoT gateways that translate OT protocols to cloud-consumable MQTT/AMQP streams, enabling data manipulation, OT command injection, and persistent access across network boundaries.

nodes.title: Exploit Gateway's Internet-Facing Services
nodes.description: Attack the cloud-connected side of the IIoT gateway — its MQTT broker credentials, web management interface, or cloud platform integration.

nodes.title: Compromise Gateway from OT Side
nodes.description: Exploit the gateway's OT-facing interfaces (Modbus, OPC-UA, BACnet) or its local management ports from within the OT network.

nodes.title: Steal MQTT/AMQP Broker Credentials
nodes.description: Extract MQTT broker credentials from the gateway's configuration file, firmware image, or cloud platform API to subscribe to all telemetry topics.

nodes.title: Exploit Web Management Interface
nodes.description: Attack the gateway's web admin panel — default credentials, command injection, or authentication bypass — to gain shell access to the embedded Linux OS.

nodes.title: Publish Spoofed Commands via MQTT Writeback
nodes.description: Use compromised MQTT credentials to publish commands on write-back topics that the gateway translates into Modbus/OPC-UA commands on the OT side.

nodes.title: OPC-UA Anonymous Session Exploitation
nodes.description: Connect to the gateway's OPC-UA server using anonymous authentication (often enabled by default) to read all tag values and write setpoints.

nodes.title: Firmware Extraction and Backdoor Implant
nodes.description: Extract the gateway's firmware via UART/SPI, reverse-engineer it, implant a persistent backdoor, and re-flash — maintaining access through factory resets.

nodes.title: Manipulate OT Telemetry Data in Transit
nodes.description: Intercept and modify sensor data as it passes through the gateway before being forwarded to the SCADA/cloud, creating a false view of the process state.

nodes.title: OPC-UA Anonymous Authentication Enabled
nodes.description: Many IIoT gateways ship with OPC-UA anonymous authentication enabled by default, allowing unauthenticated read/write access to all exposed tags.

nodes.title: Hardcoded Credentials in Gateway Firmware
nodes.description: Gateway firmware contains hardcoded service account credentials for MQTT, OPC-UA, and web management interfaces that are identical across all deployed units.

nodes.title: Gateway Bridges Cloud MQTT to OT Modbus/OPC-UA
nodes.description: The IIoT gateway's core function — translating between cloud protocols and OT protocols — makes it a high-value pivot point for crossing the IT/OT boundary in either direction.
